prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, ____ con il direttore.' (parlare, io)
explanation: When you say what you would do in someone else's place, you reach for the conditional. A regular -are verb builds it on the very stem you already use for the future (parlare gives parler-, shifting the theme vowel a to e), then adds the conditional ending -ei for io: parlerei. Keep the a (parlarei) and you have made the commonest -are slip; the stem is the same as parlerò, only the ending changes.
examiner_note: Future and conditional share the stem parler-; this item tests the -ei ending and the a-to-e shift. Pairs with the dare/stare/fare items, which do NOT shift.

prompt: Completa: 'Tu ____ di più, ma sei troppo stanco.' (lavorare)
explanation: The clause 'ma sei troppo stanco' (but you are too tired) signals something wished-for rather than real, so the verb takes the conditional. On the future stem lavorer- the conditional ending for tu is -esti: lavoreresti. Subject Tu is already shown, so you only map it to its ending; keeping the -are a (lavoraresti) is the slip.
examiner_note: Subject Tu explicit, so cue names the verb alone (cue economy, Rev 9).

prompt: Completa: 'Con più soldi, Marco ____ una casa al mare.' (comprare)
explanation: 'Con più soldi' (with more money) sets up a hypothetical, so the verb is conditional. The third-person-singular ending is the double-b -ebbe, on the future stem comprer-: comprerebbe. The doubled b is the signature of the conditional 3sg; writing a single b (comprerebe) is the spelling miss to avoid.
examiner_note: Subject Marco explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests the -ebbe double-b ending.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto vostro, ____ in centro.' (abitare, noi)
explanation: This is the conditional's signature spelling. The first-person-plural ending is -emmo with a DOUBLE m, on the future stem abiter-: abiteremmo. The future of the same verb is abiteremo with a single m, so one letter is the whole difference between 'we would live' (abiteremmo) and 'we will live' (abiteremo). Reach for two m's whenever you mean would.
examiner_note: THE signature conditional/future contrast. Single-m abiteremo (the future) is the load-bearing must_not_include; abitaremmo guards the a-shift.

prompt: Completa: 'Volentieri ____ con noi, ma sono timidi.' (cantare, loro)
explanation: 'Ma sono timidi' (but they are shy) marks the singing as hypothetical, so the verb is conditional. The third-person-plural ending is -ebbero, again with the doubled b, on the future stem canter-: canterebbero. Drop one b (canterebero) and you have the common 3pl misspelling.
examiner_note: Subject pro-dropped, so cue keeps the person (cantare, loro). Tests the -ebbero double-b ending.

prompt: Completa: 'Se potessimo, ____ subito a casa.' (tornare, noi)

translation.reveal: tornare - to return / to go back

explanation: The if-clause 'se potessimo' (if we could) calls for the conditional in the main clause. On the future stem torner- the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: torneremmo. Compare the future torneremo with one m: the doubled m is exactly what marks 'we would return' apart from 'we will return'.
examiner_note: Second dedicated double-m item, different verb, to drive the hot spot. Future torneremo guarded.

prompt: Completa: 'Grazie, ____ volentieri un tè.' (prendere, io)
explanation: Accepting an offer politely ('grazie, volentieri') takes the conditional. An -ere verb drops only the final -e of the infinitive to give the future stem prender-, with nothing dropped from inside, and adds the io ending -ei: prenderei. Cutting the stem down to prendr- (prendrei) wrongly treats a regular -ere verb as if it syncopated.
examiner_note: prendrei guards against false syncope (treating a regular -ere verb like avere/dovere).

prompt: Completa: 'Marco ti ____ una lettera, se avesse tempo.' (scrivere)
explanation: The if-clause 'se avesse tempo' (if he had time) makes the writing hypothetical, so the verb is conditional. On the regular -ere stem scriver- the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe: scriverebbe. Keep the full stem (not scrivr-) and double the b; a single b (scriverebe) is the usual slip.
examiner_note: Subject Marco explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests -ebbe; scrivrebbe guards false syncope.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, ____ il contratto due volte.' (leggere, noi)
explanation: Advice framed as 'al posto tuo' (in your place) takes the conditional. The noi ending is the double-m -emmo on the regular -ere stem legger-: leggeremmo. The matching future is leggeremo with one m, so the doubled m is the only thing separating 'we would read' from 'we will read'.
examiner_note: Future leggeremo is the load-bearing guard for the double-m.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto mio, voi cosa ____, il treno o l'aereo?' (prendere)

translation.reveal: prendere - to take / to catch (a train)

explanation: Asking what someone would choose in your place ('al posto mio') takes the conditional. The voi ending is -este on the regular -ere stem prender-: prendereste. Subject Voi is already in the sentence, so map it to its ending; keep the whole stem rather than cutting it to prendr-.
examiner_note: Subject Voi explicit, cue names the verb alone. Covers the 2pl -este ending.

prompt: Completa: 'Con un po' di calma, ____ meglio la lezione.' (capire, io)
explanation: Capire takes the -isc- insert in the present (capisco), but the conditional has no -isc- at all: it is built on the future stem capir-, which keeps the infinitive's i, plus the io ending -ei, giving capirei. Carrying the present's insert into the conditional (capiscirei) is the slip this item guards against.
examiner_note: Suppressed: a 'Regular -ire' breadcrumb would tell the learner capire is regular here (no -isc-), the very thing tested. capiscirei is the diagnostic miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Da solo, il lavoro non ____ mai.' (finire)
explanation: Finire also takes -isc- in the present (finisco), yet the conditional drops it entirely: the stem is finir-, and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving finirebbe. Two misses lurk here, so the item guards both: the present's -isc- dragged in (finiscirebbe) and the single b (finirebe).
examiner_note: Suppressed (same reasoning as capirei). Subject 'il lavoro' explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests no-isc- and the -ebbe double-b.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto vostro, ____ subito.' (partire, noi)
explanation: Partire is a plain -ire verb (no -isc-), so the conditional is built on partir- with the double-m noi ending -emmo: partiremmo. The future is partiremo with a single m, so the doubled m again carries the whole 'would' versus 'will' distinction.
examiner_note: Plain -ire (no -isc- association), so left VISIBLE. Future partiremo guards the double-m.

prompt: Completa: 'Molti ____ restare a casa, se potessero.' (preferire)
explanation: Preferire takes -isc- in the present (preferisco), but not in the conditional: on the stem preferir- the 3pl ending is -ebbero, giving preferirebbero. The if-clause 'se potessero' fixes the conditional. The item guards both the carried-in -isc- (preferiscirebbero) and the single-b 3pl (preferirebero).
examiner_note: Suppressed: -isc- verb, breadcrumb would leak that the conditional is regular here. Subject 'Molti' explicit, cue names the verb alone.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, ____ un altro lavoro.' (cercare, io)

translation.reveal: cercare - to look for / to search

explanation: Cercare ends in -care, whose hard /k/ sound has to be protected before the front-vowel conditional stem; Italian does this by inserting an h. So the stem is cercher- (just as in the future cercherò), plus the io ending -ei: cercherei. Leave the h out (cercerei) and the c would soften to /tʃ/, which is the miss to avoid.
examiner_note: Suppressed: breadcrumb names the rule-output (add h), the skill under test.

prompt: Completa: 'Chi ____ il conto, al posto suo?' (pagare)
explanation: Pagare ends in -gare, so the hard /g/ is kept before the conditional stem by inserting an h: the stem is pagher- (as in the future pagherò), and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving pagherebbe. The item guards both the dropped h (pagerebbe) and the single b (pagherebe).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject 'Chi' explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests h-insert and -ebbe.

prompt: Completa: 'Volentieri ____ a carte, ma è già tardi.' (giocare, noi)
explanation: Giocare ends in -care, so the conditional inserts an h to keep the hard /k/: the stem is giocher-, and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo, giving giocheremmo. Two things have to be right at once, so the item guards both: the single-m future giocheremo and the dropped-h gioceremmo.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Combines the h-insert with the double-m; future giocheremo guards the single m.

prompt: Completa: 'I professori ti ____ tutto, se glielo chiedessi.' (spiegare)
explanation: Spiegare ends in -gare, so an h goes in to keep the hard /g/: the stem is spiegher-, and the 3pl ending is -ebbero, giving spiegherebbero. The conditional is fixed by the if-clause 'se glielo chiedessi'. The item guards the dropped h (spiegerebbero) and the single-b 3pl (spiegherebero).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject 'I professori' explicit, cue names the verb alone.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, ____ subito.' (cominciare, io)
explanation: Cominciare ends in -ciare, whose i is there only to soften the c before a and o; before the conditional's front vowel the soft sound is automatic, so the redundant i drops. The stem is therefore comincer- (as in the future comincerò), plus -ei: comincerei. Keeping the i (comincierei) is the slip.
examiner_note: Suppressed: breadcrumb names the rule-output (drop the i).

prompt: Completa: 'Senza un buon motivo, lui non ____ mai la carne.' (mangiare)
explanation: Mangiare ends in -giare, so the redundant i drops before the conditional stem: the stem is manger-, and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving mangerebbe. The item guards both the kept i (mangierebbe) and the single b (mangerebe).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject 'lui' explicit, cue names the verb alone.

prompt: Completa: 'Con più tempo libero, ____ molto di più.' (viaggiare, noi)
explanation: Viaggiare ends in -giare, so the redundant i drops: the stem is viagger-, and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo, giving viaggeremmo. Two adjustments meet here, so the item guards both: the single-m future viaggeremo and the kept-i viaggieremmo.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Combines i-drop with the double-m; future viaggeremo guards the single m.

prompt: Completa: 'Voi ____ davvero tutto per un sogno?' (lasciare)

translation.reveal: lasciare - to leave / to give up

explanation: Lasciare ends in -sciare, so the redundant i drops before the conditional stem lascer-, and the voi ending -este attaches: lascereste. Subject Voi is already shown; keep the i out (not lasciereste).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject Voi explicit, cue names the verb alone. Covers the 2pl -este.

prompt: Completa: 'Con più tempo libero, ____ voglia di studiare.' (avere, io)
explanation: Avere drops its theme vowel in the future and conditional, leaving the root directly before the -r- of the ending; this vowel-dropping is called syncopation. So the stem is avr- (exactly the future avrò's stem), plus the io ending -ei: avrei. Keep the vowel (averei) and you have undone the syncopation, the characteristic miss.
examiner_note: Suppressed: 'syncopated stems' breadcrumb hands over that the vowel drops. avrei is also the avere-auxiliary for the condizionale passato.

prompt: Completa: 'Scusa, ____ aiutarmi un momento?' (potere, tu)

translation.reveal: potere - can / to be able to

explanation: A softened request ('scusa, ... un momento?') is one of the conditional's commonest jobs. Potere syncopates: the theme vowel drops to give the stem potr- (as in potrò), and the tu ending is -esti, giving potresti. Keeping the vowel (poteresti) is the slip. Potresti is one of the highest-frequency conditional forms a learner meets.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person (potere, tu). High-frequency polite form.

prompt: Completa: 'Secondo me, Marco ____ riposare di più.' (dovere)
explanation: Giving advice ('secondo me, ... dovrebbe') is classic conditional territory. Dovere syncopates: the vowel drops to the stem dovr- (as in dovrò), and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving dovrebbe. The item guards the kept vowel (doverebbe) and the single b (dovrebe).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject Marco explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests syncope plus -ebbe.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto vostro, ci ____ subito.' (andare, noi)
explanation: Andare syncopates to the stem andr- (as in andrò), and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: andremmo. Here two irregular features meet: the dropped vowel and the doubled m. The future is andremo with one m, so the doubled m marks 'we would go' apart from 'we will go'; keeping the vowel gives anderemmo.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Combines syncope with the double-m; future andremo is the load-bearing guard.

prompt: Completa: 'Da solo non ____ proprio cosa fare.' (sapere, io)

translation.reveal: sapere - to know / to know how to

explanation: Sapere syncopates: the theme vowel drops to give the stem sapr- (as in saprò), and the io ending -ei attaches: saprei. Keeping the vowel (saperei) is the miss. Saprei sits in many everyday phrases ('non saprei', I wouldn't know).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person (sapere, io).

prompt: Completa: 'Con gli occhiali nuovi, ____ molto meglio.' (vedere, loro)
explanation: Vedere syncopates to the stem vedr- (as in vedrò), and the 3pl ending is -ebbero, giving vedrebbero. The item guards both the kept vowel (vederebbero) and the single-b 3pl (vedrebero).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person (vedere, loro).

prompt: Completa: '____ avere il conto, per favore?' (potere, io)

translation.reveal: potere - can / to be able to

explanation: Potrei ('could I / I could') is among the first conditional forms learners meet, as a fixed polite phrase. Potere syncopates to the stem potr- (as in potrò), and the io ending -ei gives potrei. Keeping the vowel (poterei) is the slip.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Tagged A2 because potrei is met early as a fixed polite request, though the system is B1. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person.

prompt: Completa: '____ un caffè, per favore.' (volere, io)

translation.reveal: volere - to want / to would like

explanation: Vorrei ('I would like') is the single most frequent conditional form in everyday Italian, the polite way to ask for something. Volere first drops its theme vowel, then the left-over consonant assimilates into a doubled r; this matching of one sound to its neighbour is assimilation. The stem is vorr- (as in vorrò), plus -ei: vorrei. Writing one r (vorei) is the signature miss.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Highest-frequency conditional form; tagged A2 as a fixed polite phrase. vorei (single-r) is the load-bearing guard.

prompt: Completa: '____ qualcosa da bere?' (volere, tu)

translation.reveal: volere - to want / to would like

explanation: Offering something politely ('would you like ...?') uses the conditional of volere. The stem assimilates to the doubled-r vorr- (as in vorrò), and the tu ending is -esti: vorresti. One r (voresti) is the slip; keeping the whole infinitive (voleresti) misses the assimilation entirely.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person (volere, tu). Single-r voresti guarded.

prompt: Completa: 'Volentieri ____ alla festa, ma non possiamo.' (venire, noi)
explanation: Venire takes the assimilated double-r stem verr- (as in verrò), and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: verremmo. This form carries two doublings at once, so two misses lurk: a single r (veremmo) loses the assimilation, and a single m (verremo) is actually the future. 'Ma non possiamo' fixes the conditional.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Tests double-r AND double-m together; guards veremmo (single-r) and verremo (single-m future).

prompt: Completa: 'Lui ____ il segreto, se glielo chiedessi.' (tenere)
explanation: Tenere takes the double-r stem terr- (as in terrò), and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving terrebbe. Two doublings again: the item guards the single r (terebbe) and the un-assimilated whole stem (tenerebbe).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject 'Lui' explicit, cue names the verb alone. Single-r terebbe guarded; also tests -ebbe.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, ____ a casa stasera.' (rimanere, io)
explanation: Rimanere takes the double-r stem rimarr- (as in rimarrò), plus the io ending -ei: rimarrei. A single r (rimarei) loses the assimilation; keeping the whole infinitive (rimanerei) misses it altogether.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person (rimanere, io). Single-r rimarei guarded.

prompt: Completa: 'Con questo caldo, ____ litri d'acqua.' (bere, loro)
explanation: Bere builds its future and conditional on the short stem ber-, which assimilates to the double-r berr- (as in berrò), with the 3pl ending -ebbero: berrebbero. Note the stem is ber-, not the bev- of the present (bevo): a bev- form (beverebbero) is wrong here, as is the single r (berebbero).
examiner_note: Suppressed. bere's cond/future stem is the short ber- (not present bev-); beverebbero guards that, berebbero guards the single-r.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, ____ più prudente.' (essere, io)
explanation: Essere has no theme vowel to work from, so its future and conditional use an entirely separate stem, sar- (the same one behind sarò); a stem unrelated to its infinitive like this is called suppletive. The io ending -ei gives sarei. Building from the infinitive (esserei) is the slip.
examiner_note: Suppressed. sarei is also the essere-auxiliary for the condizionale passato; esserei guards the infinitive-built miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Senza di te, tutto ____ più difficile.' (essere)
explanation: Essere uses the suppletive stem sar- (as in sarà), and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving sarebbe. The item guards the infinitive-built form (esserebbe) and the single b (sarebe).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject 'tutto' explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests the suppletive stem plus -ebbe.

prompt: Completa: 'Con un po' di aiuto, ____ felici di partecipare.' (essere, noi)
explanation: Essere uses the suppletive stem sar-, and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: saremmo. The future is saremo with one m, so the doubled m separates 'we would be' from 'we will be'; building from the infinitive gives esseremmo.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Future saremo is the load-bearing double-m guard.

prompt: Completa: 'Loro ____ certamente contenti di vederti.' (essere)
explanation: Essere uses the suppletive stem sar-, and the 3pl ending is -ebbero, giving sarebbero. The item guards the single-b 3pl (sarebero) and the infinitive-built form (esserebbero).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject Loro explicit, cue names the verb alone.

prompt: Completa: 'Al posto tuo, non lo ____.' (fare, io)
explanation: Most -are verbs shift their theme vowel a to e in the conditional, but dare, stare and fare are the exception: they keep the -ar-. So fare gives the stem far- (as in farò), plus the io ending -ei: farei. Applying the usual shift (ferei) is exactly the trap this set is built to expose.
examiner_note: Suppressed. The counter-example to the -are a-to-e shift; ferei (over-applied shift) is the diagnostic miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Mi ____ un favore, per cortesia?' (fare, tu)

translation.reveal: fare - to do / to make / to do (a favour)

explanation: Asking a favour politely uses the conditional. Fare keeps the -ar- against the usual shift, giving the stem far- (as in farò), and the tu ending is -esti: faresti. Shifting the vowel (feresti) is the slip.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person (fare, tu).

prompt: Completa: 'Lei ti ____ una mano, se potesse.' (dare)
explanation: Dare keeps the -ar- like fare and stare, giving the stem dar- (as in darò), and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe: darebbe. The item guards both the over-applied shift (derebbe) and the single b (darebe).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject Lei explicit, cue names the verb alone. Tests keep--ar- plus -ebbe.

prompt: Completa: 'In una casa più grande, ____ molto meglio.' (stare, noi)

translation.reveal: stare - to stay / to be (of wellbeing)

explanation: Stare keeps the -ar-, giving the stem star- (as in starò), and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: staremmo. The future is staremo with one m, so the doubled m carries the 'would' sense; applying the shift gives steremmo.
examiner_note: Suppressed. Combines keep--ar- with the double-m; future staremo guards the single m.

prompt: Completa: 'Cosa ____ i tuoi genitori al posto mio?' (fare)
explanation: Fare keeps the -ar-, giving the stem far-, and the 3pl ending is -ebbero: farebbero. The item guards the over-applied shift (ferebbero) and the single-b 3pl (farebero).
examiner_note: Suppressed. Subject 'i tuoi genitori' explicit, cue names the verb alone.

prompt: Completa: 'Con un'ora in più, ____ tutto il lavoro.' (finire, io)
explanation: To say what you would have done, Italian uses the compound conditional, the conditional perfect: the conditional of the auxiliary plus the past participle. Finire takes avere, whose conditional is avrei, with the participle finito: avrei finito. The participle stays invariable with avere here (not finita), and the auxiliary is the syncopated avrei, not averei.
examiner_note: Single markpoint: avere is invariable here (no preceding DOP), so this tests the compound formation as one skill. Guards wrong aux (sarei), un-syncopated aux (averei), and false agreement (finita).

prompt: Completa: 'Maria aveva detto che ____ volentieri alla festa.' (andare)
explanation: Where English says 'she said she would come', Italian puts the reported future-in-the-past into the conditional perfect: the conditional of the auxiliary plus the participle. Andare takes essere, whose conditional 3sg is sarebbe, and with essere the participle agrees with the subject Maria, giving andata: sarebbe andata. The auxiliary is essere (sarebbe), not avere.
examiner_note: Reported future-in-the-past. Two markpoints (criterion 8): the conditional essere auxiliary (this tree) and the f.sg participle agreement (PP tree). Subject Maria explicit, cue names the verb alone. Marks 2.

prompt: Completa: 'Maria, senza la sveglia non ____ in tempo.' (partire)
explanation: This is a counterfactual about the past ('without the alarm you would not have left in time'), which takes the conditional perfect. Partire takes essere, whose conditional 2sg is saresti, and the participle agrees with the addressee Maria, giving partita: saresti partita. The auxiliary is essere (saresti), not avere.
examiner_note: Address to a feminine subject (Maria) pins f.sg agreement. Two markpoints: conditional essere auxiliary + f.sg participle agreement. Cue names the verb alone.

prompt: Completa: 'Con il treno veloce, ____ molto prima.' (arrivare, noi)

markpoints.label: auxiliary saremmo (essere cond, double-m NOT future saremo)

explanation: A counterfactual about the past ('we would have arrived much earlier') takes the conditional perfect. Arrivare takes essere, whose conditional 1pl is the double-m saremmo, and the participle agrees with the plural subject noi, giving arrivati: saremmo arrivati. Note the auxiliary keeps the doubled m (saremmo, not the future saremo) and the verb is essere, not avere.
examiner_note: Combines the double-m conditional auxiliary with m.pl agreement. Two markpoints. Subject pro-dropped, cue keeps the person. Future saremo guards the double-m on the auxiliary.

prompt: Completa: 'Aveva promesso che ci ____ una mano.' (dare)
explanation: Another reported future-in-the-past ('he had promised he would give us a hand'), so the conditional perfect. Dare takes avere, whose conditional 3sg is avrebbe, with the participle dato: avrebbe dato. The 'ci' here is an indirect object (to us), not a preceding direct object, so the participle stays invariable (dato, never data), and the auxiliary is avere (avrebbe), not essere.
examiner_note: Single markpoint: avere with an indirect object stays invariable, so this is one skill. Guards wrong aux (sarebbe), un-syncopated aux (averebbe), and false agreement (data).
